metadata.summary: Tool built on top of West for managing nRF Connect SDK projects.

documentation:
# East - tool for building NCS/Zephyr applications

East is a command line meta-tool, useful for creating, managing, and deploying
[Zephyr] or [nRF Connect SDK] (NCS) projects.

[zephyr]: https://docs.zephyrproject.org/latest/
[nrf connect sdk]:
  https://developer.nordicsemi.com/nRF_Connect_SDK/doc/latest/nrf/introduction.html

It is built on top of Zephyr's RTOS meta-tool [West] and Nordic's [nRF Connect
Toolchain Manager].

[west]: https://github.com/zephyrproject-rtos/west
[nrf connect toolchain manager]:
  https://github.com/NordicSemiconductor/pc-nrfconnect-toolchain-manager

## Reasoning behind East

There are several reasons why someone would like to create yet another tool on
top of `west`:

- Working on several projects at once means using different versions of the NCS
  repository and different versions of the toolchain. Managing these differences
  is not a trivial task.
- There is no reproducible build guarantee between the developer's machines.
  Slight differences between tool versions can manifest into hard-to-find bugs.
- Creating GitHub releases manually takes ages as you have to run the build
  process for every combination of the board, application, build variant, etc.
  and properly rename the release binary. The release procedure gets longer with
  every addition of new hardware and build variation option.

East automates the above tasks and tries to make the developer more productive.

## Key features

- Automated detection and installation of tooling required for NCS projects.
- Common `west` commands used for the development, such as `build`, `flash`,
  etc.
- Sandboxed development environment, thanks to the nRF Connect Toolchain
  Manager, every `build`, `flash`, etc. command runs inside of its toolchain
  environment.
- Automated process of generating release artefacts for your entire project, no
  matter the number of applications, samples or boards.
- Support for build types, which is integrated into the usual build process.
- RTT utility commands to connect and see the RTT stream.

## Documentation

`docs` directory contains several markdown documents about East:

- [Getting stared] - Quickly setup an example project and get it building with
  East.
- [How East works] - How East works under the hood and what to expect from it.
- [Configuration] - How to configure build types and release procedure via
  `east.yml` file.
- [Development guide] - How to setup development environment for working on
  East.

[getting stared]: docs/getting_started.md
[how east works]: docs/how_east_works.md
[configuration]: docs/configuration.md
[development guide]: docs/development_guide.md

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.build_type_flag.is_child_in_parent
documentation:
Check if the child path is in parent path
    parent (): path
    child ():  path
Returns: True if yes, otherwise False.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.build_type_flag._construct_required_cmake_args
documentation:
Constructs required cmake args from conf_files. Adds board specific conf file if it
is found.
Path prefix is applied, for cases where sample inherits a list conf_files.

    conf_files (List(str)):     List of conf files
    board (str):                West board name
    path_prefix (str):          Path prefix added to conf files

Returns:
    Cmake args

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.build_type_flag._check_previous_build
documentation:
Checks if rebuild is needed by looking at the contents of the
last_build_type_flag file.

Args:
    east ():            East context
    build_dir ():       Location of build_dir
    build_type_str ():  Build type in string format

Returns:
    one of the following strings:
    - "nobuild" if there is no build directory.
    - "rebuild" if rebuild is needed.
    - "just_build" if rebuild is not needed.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.build_type_flag.no_build_type_msg
documentation: Returns a message that a given build type does not exist in east.yml.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.build_type_flag.construct_extra_cmake_arguments
documentation:
Construct extra cmake arguments for west build command.

This function will construct cmake_arguments (specifically values for OVERLAY_CONFIG
and CONF_FILE defines), based on:
* given build type
* given board
* contents of east.yml file
* contents of build folder if found

The function behaviour is described:
* in docs/configuration.md document - That document basically describes how should
east build behave.
* in tests/test_build_type.py - Tests the behaviour east build command in a
variety of different scenarios.

    east ():            East context
    build_type ():      Build type given from the east build
    board ():           Board given from the east build
    build_dir ():       Location of build_dir, if None then "./build" is used

Returns:  Tuple of strings
            First string are extra cmake arguments for west build command should be
            placed after `--` in west command
            Second string is diagnostic message that can be printed. It might not be
            given so it should be checked first.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.cortex_commands.cortex_debug
documentation:
Create a configuration file for the [bold green]Cortex Debug[/] VScode extension.

     
    

Running this command inside the application project directory creates a [bold].vscode/launch.json[/] file that configures the [bold green]Cortex Debug[/] for debugging that project.

     
    

If the command is run without any extra options then it will try to infer the required info from the [bold]runners.yaml[/] file. If that is not desired then the --device, --gdb and --elf options can be used to provide info directly.

     
    

SVD files for Nordic chips are determined automatically. You can override this with the --svd-file option.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.check_for_codechecker
documentation: Check if codechecker is installed and available in the path.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.check_for_build_folder
documentation: Check that build dir exists.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.check_for_compile_commands_json
documentation: Check that compile_commands.json exists in the build dir.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.check_for_url
documentation: Checks if URL is provided.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.cleanup_compile_commands_json
documentation:
Remove GCC specific arguments from compile_commands.json.

Otherwise clangsa fails to analyze the files.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.check_for_codechecker_config_yaml
documentation: Check that codechecker_config.yaml exists in the project's root dir.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.detect_problematic_zephyr_macros
documentation:
Return true if a problematic Zephyr macros is flagged by clang-tidy diagnostics.

Macros are always connected to a specific description.
Different problematic macros can have the same description.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.var_never_read_before_disabled_macro_found
documentation:
Returns true if clang-tidy diagnostic is about value being stored to a variable,
and not being read, but this variable is later passed into some kind of a disabled
macro, such as __ASSERT* or LOG_* macro.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.sizeof_on_pointer_type_found
documentation:
Returns true if clang-tidy diagnostic is about sizeof() being called on a
pointer, inside a LOG_*, EVENT_LOG* or APP_EVENT_MANAGER_LOG macro.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.cleanup_plist_files
documentation: Cleanup generated plist files by removing diagnostics that are not useful.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.create_skip_file
documentation:
Create skip file for CodeChecker.

Generated skip file skips analysis of Zephyr, NCS, external repositories and
contents of any build folder inside the project's root dir.

We are skipping build folder because it contains generated .c files that are not in
other folders.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.create_codecheckerfile
documentation:
Create a file in the build folder that contains all information needed for the
codechecker store command.

The intention is that east codechecker store reads the file and uses it for
creating the metadata of the run when storing to the server.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.get_metadata_from_codecheckerfile
documentation:
Read the codecheckerfile.json file and construct the name and the tag, intended
to be used by codechecker store command.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_helpers.get_endpoint
documentation: Get the endpoint from the project's git remote origin.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.clean
documentation:
Clean the build folder in current directory.

     
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.build
documentation:
Build firmware in the current directory.

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west build[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it. To learn more about possible [magenta bold]west build[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.


    

After the build step, if the file [bold]compile_commands.json[/] is found in build dir it will be copied to the project and top west directory. This makes job of locating this file easier for [bold yellow]clangd[/].


    

To pass additional arguments to the [bold]CMake[/] invocation performed by the [magenta
    bold]west build[/], pass them after a [bold white]"--"[/] at the end of the command line.

    

If the source dir is listed in the [bold yellow]east.yml[/] then [bold yellow]build type[/] functionality is enabled. East will add a group of [bold]Kconfig[/] fragment files to the build as specified by the selected build type and [bold yellow]east.yml[/] file. See [bold]docs/configuration.md[/] for more info.

    

[bold]Note:[/] When using build types functionality make sure to not pass CONF_FILE and OVERLAY_CONFIG variables to the [bold]CMake[/].
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.create_build_command_from_commandline
documentation:
Helper for creating a build command.

It parses raw_args (which is a list) and creates two objects:

    - build_cmd: a string with the build command, intended to be given to run_west()
    - opts: an object with parsed arguments

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.find_source_dir
documentation: Helper for finding the source directory in the raw_args.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.flash
documentation:
Flash binary to the board's flash.

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west flash[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible [magenta bold]west flash[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.


    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.bypass
documentation:
Bypass any set of commands directly to the [magenta bold]nrfutil toolchain-manager[/].

     
    

Passing any set of commands after double dash [bold]--[/] will pass them directly to
    the [bold magenta]nrfutil toolchain-manager[/] executable.

    

Those commands will run in the context of the isolated environment, which is provided by the executable.

    

Example:

    

Command [bold]east bypass -- west build -b nrf52840dk_nrf52840[/]
    

becomes [bold]west build -b nrf52840dk_nrf52840[/]



    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.debug
documentation:
Connect to the board, flash the program, and start a debugging session.

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west debug[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible [magenta bold]west debug[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.

    

[bold]Note:[/] Add --tui flag to use Text User Interface.
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.attach
documentation:
Like "east debug", but doesn't reflash the program.

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west attach[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible [magenta bold]west attach[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.

    

[bold]Note:[/] Add --tui flag to use Text User Interface.
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.twister
documentation:
Run Twister, a test runner tool.

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west twister[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible Twister arguments and options use --extra-help flag.


    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

classes.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.SpecialCommand

methods.name: east.workspace_commands.basic_commands.SpecialCommand.parse_args
documentation: Parse the args and store a copy of them in the context.
Special command that allows to access the raw args.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.check
documentation:
Run [magenta bold]Codechecker[/] analysis for a built project.

     
    

Expects that a CodeChecker config file (codechecker_config.yaml) exists in the
    project's root dir. That file is used to configure the analysis.
    See https://github.com/Ericsson/codechecker/tree/master/docs/config_file.md for more
    information. Run east codechecker example-config to see a suggested config file.

    

Results of analysis are printed in the terminal by default. If --html flag is given, a html report is generated instead.

    

Between analysis and parsing of the results, the intermediary plist files are by default cleaned up to remove diagnostics that are not useful. To skip this step, use --dont-cleanup-plist flag.

    

Currently removed diagnostics are:

    

- [bold magenta]Ineffective bitwise and operation[/], caused by [bold cyan]LOG_*[/] macros.
    

- [bold magenta]Conditional operator with identical true and false expressions[/], caused by [bold cyan]LOG_*[/], [bold cyan]SHELL_CMD_*[/], [bold cyan]APP_EVENT_MANAGER_*[/] macros.
    

- [bold magenta]Value stored to 'variable' is never read or used[/], but is actually later used in disabled [bold cyan]__ASSERT*[/] or [bold cyan]LOG_*[/] macros.
    

- [bold magenta]Comparison of integers of different signs: 'int' and 'unsigned int'[/], caused by [bold cyan]INIT_OBJ_RES*[/] macros.
    

- [bold magenta]Missing field 'help' initializer[/], caused by [bold cyan]SHELL_SUBCMD*[/] macros.

    

By default, a generated skip file is used to skip needless analysis of the Zephyr, NCS and external repositories. If that is not desired, you can provide your own skip file with the --skip-file flag, check the link for more info: https://codechecker.readthedocs.io/en/latest/analyzer/user_guide/#skip


    

[bold]Note:[/] This command expects that the project's build folder contains compile_commands.json

    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.fixit
documentation:
Apply fixes suggested by the [magenta bold]Codechecker[/].

     
    

Some analyzers may suggest some automatic bugfixes. Most of the times these are style issues which can be fixed easily. This command handles the listing and application of these automatic fixes.

    

[bold]Note:[/] This command should be ran after the [bold cyan]east codechecker check[/] command.
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.store
documentation:
Store the results of the [magenta bold]Codechecker[/] analysis to a server.

     
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command should be ran after the [bold cyan]east codechecker check[/] command.
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.example_config
documentation: Show example [bold magenta]codechecker_config.yaml[/] file.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.bypass
documentation:
Directly run any [magenta bold]CodeChecker[/] command.

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]codechecker[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible [magenta bold]codechecker[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.


    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.servdiff
documentation:
Compare local analysis against the last server analysis.

     
    

Use one of the --new, --resolved, --unresolved flags to specify how to compare.

    

[bold]Note:[/] This command should be ran after the [bold cyan]east codechecker check[/] command.

    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.codechecker_commands.codechecker
documentation:
Command with several subcommands related to [magenta bold]CodeChecker[/].

     
    

If running CodeChecker inside continuous integration environment, run [bold cyan]export EAST_CODECHECKER_CI_MODE=1[/] before running any [bold cyan]east codechecker[/] commands.
    This will make [bold cyan]east[/] use the [magenta bold]CodeChecker[/] executable that is on the system path instead of the one in the tooling directory. The system provided [magenta bold]CodeChecker[/] will normally also want to use the system provided clang, clang-tidy and cppcheck.

     
    

This way users can leverage the programs provided by continuous integration
    environment and not by [bold cyan]east[/], which is usually faster due to caching.

variables.name: east.east_context.RICH_CONSOLE_ENABLE_MARKUP
documentation:
Convenience dicts for storing settings that are identical across Click's commands and
groups.

classes.name: east.east_context.EastContext

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.__init__
documentation:
Create a new context object.

Args:

: echo (bool): If True `.run` prints the command string to local stdout prior
  to executing it. Default: `False`.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.chdir
documentation:
Change directory.

self ():
path (str): Relative or absolute path of the directory to change to.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.print
documentation:
Prints to the console.

Internally it uses Console object, so whatever Console can do, this function can
also do.

Full documentation here:
https://rich.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/console.html#rich.console.Console.print

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.print_info
documentation:
Prints a message, with many rich settings disabled, with magnify icon
prepended. Suitable for printing info messages that should not be formatted.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.print_markdown
documentation:
Interprets given object (string) as Markdown style text and prints it to the
console.

Internally it uses Markdown object, so whatever Markdown can do, this function
can also do.
https://rich.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference/markdown.html#rich.markdown.Markdown

Bonus thing: Any kwargs that are passed to it are correctly sorted and either
passed to the Markdown object or to the internal self.print function which uses
Console object.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.exit
documentation: Exit program with given return_code.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.run
documentation:
Executes given command in shell as a process.

This is a blocking call, process needs to finish before this command can return;

Args:
    command (str):  Command to execute.

    exit_on_error (str):    If true the program is exited if the return code of
                            the ran command is not 0.

    return_output (bool):   Return stdout. Note that this will mean that there
                            might be no colorcodes in the terminal output and
                            no strerr, due to piping.

    silent (bool):          Do not print command's output.

    ignore_sigint (bool):   If true it does not pass SIGINT to the run process.
                            This means that the run process will handle SIGINT
                            by itself. This is useful for running gdb.

Returns:
    Dict with two keys is always returned:
        output(str):        Contains stdout of the process that run, if
                            return_output is True, otherwise empty string.
        returncode(int):    Return code of the process that run

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.run_west
documentation:
Run wrapper which should be used when executing commands with west tool.

If toolchain for the detected ncs version is installed then west through
nrfutil toolchain-manager is used. If it is not installed then west is used
directly.

Args:
    west_command (str):     west command to execute
    kwargs:                 Anything that is supported by .run method

Returns:
    Check .run

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.enter_manager_shell
documentation: Enters nrfutil toolchain-manager shell using detected NCS version.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.run_manager
documentation:
Executes a command with nrfutil toolchain-manager executable.

This is not suitable to be used with a type of a 'launch -- <command>' command.
For that run_cmd_in_manager should be used.

Args:
    command (str):      Command to execute.
    kwargs:             Anything that is supported by .run method.

Returns:
    Check .run

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.run_cmd_in_manager
documentation:
Run an arbitrary command through nrfutil toolchain-manager.

This method should be used when passing any arbitrary command, like west command.

Args:
    command (str):          Command to execute.
    exit_on_error (bool):   If true the program is exited if the return code of
                            the ran command is not 0.
    **kwargs:               Anything that is supported by .run method.

Returns:
    Check .run

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.check_exe
documentation:
Checks if the given executable can be found by the which command.

If on_fail_exit is true it exits the program.

Args:
    exe (str):              executable to find
    on_fail_exit (bool):    If true it exits cli on exit

Returns:
    True if given executable was found, false otherwise.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.check_version
documentation:
Checks for version of provided exe program and compares it against
provided one.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.pre_workspace_command_check
documentation:
This function contains a list of checks that every workspace (not system)
command should call before executing its actual command.

In current implementation it does general things:
* Asserts that workspace command was run from the west workspace
* Tries to load east.yml
* Tries to determine the NCS version that is used in the project.
* Tries to find the version of the toolchain in the nrfutil toolchain-manager.

This function esentially tries to answer the question: should the underlying
west command be passed to the nrfutil toolchain-manager or directly to the
west.

Args:
    ignore_uninstalled_ncs (bool):  When true, do not exit if detected
                                    NCS version is not installed by the
                                    toolchain-manager. Workspace commands such
                                    as build, flash, clean should set this to
                                    False. install toolchain command should set
                                    this to True.

    ignore_unsupported_ncs (bool):  When true, do not exit if detected
                                    NCS version is not supported by the
                                    toolchain-manager. Workspace commands such
                                    as build, flash, clean should set this to
                                    True. install toolchain command should set
                                    this to False.

    check_only_west_workspace (bool): When true, only check if we are in the
                                      west Workspace, do not check for the rest
                                      of the things. This is useful for the
                                      codechecker command, which should be run
                                      inside the west workspace, but does not
                                      need nrf toolchain manager.

methods.name: east.east_context.EastContext.check_for_new_east_version
documentation:
Occasionally check if there is a new version of east available.

If there is print a message to the user.

Context-aware API wrapper & state-passing object.

EastContext object needs to be created in the top level cli command that groups all
other commands. That object then needs to be passed to every other subcommand.

Specifically, the class offers wrappers for core API calls (such as `.run`)
which take into account CLI parser flags, configuration files, and/or
changes made at runtime.

functions.name: east.east_yml.format_east_yml_load_error_msg
documentation:
Format error message for EastYmlLoadError.

Use this to format error messages that happen when trying to load east.yml

functions.name: east.east_yml.load_east_yml
documentation:
Try to load east.yml. If that succeeds validate it.

    project_dir (str): Path to project directory, where east.yml should be located.

Returns:
    dict with east.yml contents if east.yml is found and correctly validated. If it
    can not be found it returns None.

classes.name: east.east_yml.EastYmlLoadError
documentation: Some error happened when trying to load east.yml.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.create_artefact_name
documentation:
Create an artefact name.

Board might be in form <west_board>@<hv_version>, in that case we modify it to fit
the artefact name.

We also add git hash at the end if the build was not done on the clean tagged
commit.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.create_art_dest_and_src_dir
documentation: Create destination and source directories for the artefact.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.collect_binaries_using_sysbuild
documentation: Collect binaries from build folder that used sysbuild.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.collect_binaries_without_using_sysbuild
documentation: Collect binaries from build folder that did not use sysbuild.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.move_build_artefacts
documentation: Moves build artefacts to art_dest and renames them to art_name.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.show_job_summary
documentation: Prints a summary of jobs to be run.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.create_build_command
documentation:
Helper for creating a build command.

Returns:
    build_cmd: a string with the build command, intended to be given to run_west()

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.run_job
documentation:
Runs the job with west.

east ():            East context.
job ():             Job to run.
dry_run ():         If true then don't actually build, only show what commands
                    would run.
verbose():          If true the Cmake output is shown.
spdx_app_only():    If true then only apps are scanned for SPDX tags.

Return:
    Bool            True, if job succeeded, false if it did not.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.check_if_single_app_repo
documentation:
Return True if this repo has just a single app project in the 'app' folder.

Return False in any other case.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.non_existing_app_msg_fmt
documentation: Return a message.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.non_existing_sample_msg_fmt
documentation: Return a message.

functions.name: east.workspace_commands.release_commands.release
documentation:
Create a release folder with release artefacts.

     
    

[bold yellow]east release[/] command runs a release process consisting of a series of [bold yellow]east build[/] commands to build applications and samples listed in the [bold yellow]east.yml[/]. Created build artefacts are then renamed and placed into [bold magenta]release[/] folder in project's root directory.

    

Version number is inferred from [bold italic cyan]git describe --tags --always --long --dirty=+[/] command. If the [bold yellow]east release[/] command is run directly on a commit with a version tag (such as [bold cyan]v1.0.2[/]), and there are no local changes, then only version tag is added to the name of artefacts, otherwise the additional git hash qualifier is added. If there is no version tag then default of [bold cyan]v0.0.0[/] is used.

    

As both [bold]apps[/] and [bold]samples[/] keys in [bold yellow]east.yml[/] are optional, release process for a specific key will be skipped, if it is not present.

    

Different hardware versions of listed boards are picked up automatically from the `board` directory.

    

[bold]Note:[/] This command requires [bold yellow]east.yml[/] to function.
    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.system_commands.basic_west_commands.init
documentation:
Create a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west init[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible [magenta bold]west init[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.


    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.system_commands.basic_west_commands.update
documentation:
Update a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

     
    

Internally runs [magenta bold]west update[/] command, all given arguments are passed directly to it.

    

To learn more about possible [magenta bold]west update[/] arguments and options use --extra-help flag.


    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.system_commands.install.toolchain
documentation:
Install NCS toolchain for the current West workspace.

     
    

Determines the version of the NCS that is being used in the current West
    workspace and downloads the correct version of the toolchain for it.

    

[bold]Note:[/] This command can be only run from inside of a [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

functions.name: east.system_commands.install.nrfutil_toolchain_manager
documentation: Install [bold magenta]nrfutil toolchain-manager[/].

functions.name: east.system_commands.install.codechecker
documentation:
Install [bold magenta]CodeChecker[/] and its dependencies.

     
    

Current dependencies are [bold cyan]clang-tidy[/] and [bold cyan]clang[/], both are contained in the [bold cyan]clang+llvm[/] package.

functions.name: east.system_commands.install.install
documentation:
Install one or more tools.

     
    

Tools are always installed in the [bold magenta]~/.local/share/east/tooling[/] directory.

    

To install a single tool run [bold cyan]east install tool_name[/].
    

To install all tools run [bold cyan]east install --all[/].
    

To learn more about a tool run [bold cyan]east install tool_name --help[/].

functions.name: east.system_commands.jlink_commands.no_jlink_tool_msg
documentation: Return a message that a required JLink tool was not found.

functions.name: east.system_commands.jlink_commands.fmt_runner_error_msg
documentation: Format error message for EastJlinkDeviceLoadError.

functions.name: east.system_commands.jlink_commands.connect
documentation:
Connect to a device and create a RTT server with [bold cyan]JLinkExe[/].

     
    

RTT server will emit any RTT messages produced by the device over its dedicated port. Execute [bold magenta]east util rtt[/] command in the separate window to observe these messages.

functions.name: east.system_commands.jlink_commands.rtt
documentation:
Run a RTT client and connect a running RTT server.

     
    

Any messages that RTT server creates are printed. RTT server can be created with an [bold magenta]east util connect[/] command.

functions.name: east.__main__.util
documentation: Command with several subcommands related to utilities.

functions.name: east.__main__.cli
documentation:
[bold]East[/] is a command line meta-tool, useful for creating, managing and
    deploying [bold cyan]nRF Connect SDK[/] projects.

     
    

Want to learn what each command does?

    Run [bold]east \[command] --help[/] to show documentation for that command.

     
    

Note that commands are split into two groups:

    - [bold]Workspace:[/] Can only be run from inside of [bold yellow]West workspace[/].

    - [bold]System:[/] Can be run from anywhere.

functions.name: east.__main__.main
documentation: Main entry point for the CLI.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._install_nrfutil
documentation: Install nrfutil to a proper location.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._get_nrfutil_download_link
documentation:
Return link for the Nordic's nRF Util executable.

The link depends on the platform that the East is running on, however it will always
point to a lightweight launcher version of the nrfutil.

Check https://developer.nordicsemi.com/.pc-tools/nrfutil/ for interesting stuff.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._get_cppcheck_download_link
documentation:
Just a convenience function for getting the link for the Cppcheck source code.
This link will be updated regularly.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._install_cppcheck
documentation: Install cppcheck to a proper location.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._get_clang_download_link
documentation:
Return link for the clang+llvm binaries.

This link will be updated regularly.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._install_clang_llvm
documentation: Install clang+llvm to a proper location.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._get_codechecker_download_link
documentation:
Just a convenience function for getting the link for the CodeChecker source code.

This link will be updated regularly.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling._install_codechecker
documentation: Install codechecker to a proper location.

functions.name: east.system_commands.tooling.tool_installer
documentation: Install tools that are passed in the tool_names list.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.download_file
documentation: Download a file from the internet to the given path.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.download_files
documentation:
Download concurrently multiple files from the internet to the given directory.

Function expects a list of dicts, where each dict has a key "url" that points to the
file that should be downloaded and a key "name" that is used for naming and printing
purposes.

After all files were downloaded the function returns a list of paths to the
downloaded files in the same order as they were given.

Args:
    files_to_download (List[Dict]):     Files to download.
    dest_dir (List[Dict]):              Directory where to download the files.

Return:
    files (List[str]):      List of paths to the downloaded files.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.west_topdir
documentation:
Returns the path to the parent directory of the .west/
directory instead, where project repositories are stored.

Args:
    start (Optional[PathType]):     Directory from where to start searching, if not
                                    given current directory is used.

Returns:
    Full path to parent directory of the .west/ folder.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.get_ncs_and_project_dir
documentation:
Returns version of nrf-sdk project and absolute path to the projects directory.

This is combined, so we avoid reading .west/config file twice.

Args:
    west_dir_path (str):        Path to the parent of .west directory. It is up to
                                the caller to provide a correct value.

Returns:
    Revision string of nrf-sdk project, absolute

Raises:
    WestConfigNotFound or WestYamlNotFound

functions.name: east.helper_functions.return_dict_on_match
documentation:
Search through array of dicts and return the first one where the given key matches
the given value.
If none are found return None.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.no_toolchain_msg
documentation: Return message that informs user that toolchain is not installed.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.ncs_version_not_supported_msg
documentation: Return message that informs user that detected NCS version is not supported.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.find_board_dir
documentation:
Find the directory of the given board.

Search for directory that contains *_defconfig file with given west_board name,
(this is the same process that west uses to determine the boards).

After such directory is found, scan it and try to find what board revisions are
present and generate a list of board names and board revisions in format expected by
west build command.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.find_all_boards
documentation:
Find all possible revisions of west board.

If there are no revision specific files in the folder then just return west_board.

    east ():            East context.
    west_board (str):   Board to search for.

Returns:
    List of west boards to be used by west build -b <board>

functions.name: east.helper_functions.clean_up_extra_args
documentation:
Clean up extra args, by adding back double quotes to the define assignments.

Click argument automatically strips double quotes from anything that is given
after "--". We can not know for sure from where double quotes were removed,
however we know that CMake in at least one case requires them.
For example, double quotes are needed, if you are assigning a list of tokens to the
define value, like so: -DFILES="file1.txt;file2.txt".
Double quotes are also added if there is an assignment to a flag, like:
--some-flag=something.
If this line gets to Cmake without double quotes, it will not be parsed
correctly.

We also take care of the case where there is a space in the argument, in that case
we add double quotes around the whole argument.

    args ():    Extra args, passed after '--' as Click argument.

Returns:
    cleaned up args, already formatted as a string.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.create_artefact_name
documentation:
Create an artefact name.

Board might be in form <west_board>@<hv_version>, in that case we modify it to fit
the artefact name.

We also add git hash at the end if the build was not done on the clean tagged
commit.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.get_git_version
documentation:
Return structured output from git describe command.

Note: See help string of release function for more information.

Note to myself: If you use this function in some other place, make sure that you
patch it in the test files, otherwise pytest thinks that is the mocked call that it
should test against.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.get_jlink_param
documentation: Extract value for a given jlink param from runner.yaml content.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.find_app_build_dir
documentation: Find the build directory of the app.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.does_project_use_sysbuild
documentation: Based on the presence of domain.yaml file determine if the project uses sysbuild.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.get_device_in_runner_yaml
documentation:
Returns device flag for JLink runner from runners.yaml.

If it is not possible to do that it will raise an exception.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.get_jlink_speed_in_runner_yaml
documentation:
Returns speed flag for JLink runner from runners.yaml.

If it is not found, None is returned, so caller should handle that case.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.get_cortex_debug_params
documentation:
Get cortex debug parameters from the build directory.

Any path returned will be absolute.

Any failure to read something will result in a raised exception so this should be
called inside the try block.

functions.name: east.helper_functions.determine_svd_file
documentation:
Determine the SVD file for the given device.

Only Nordic devices are supported at the moment, unsupported devices will return
None.

classes.name: east.helper_functions.WestDirNotFound
documentation: Neither the current directory nor any parent has a west workspace.

classes.name: east.helper_functions.WestConfigNotFound
documentation: .west/config file does not exist.

classes.name: east.helper_functions.WestYmlNotFound
documentation: west.yml file does not exist.

classes.name: east.helper_functions.EastJlinkDeviceLoadError
documentation: Some error happened when trying to collect --device flag for JLink.
